item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I miss that. I am obviously suffering from some horrible disease.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Corpse Fuller even states that he wants to catch and torture the dynamic duo, which is not something I'd say through the mouth of my archnemesis.

Ah…

"The electro-thaumic waves on the cylinder do not precisely match up with these two lines. It should also be noted that SCP-3440-A1's voice abruptly changes to be significantly more enraged."

The implication here is that this is not what Icky said, but rather Fuller himself managing to push through a little bit.

As for them getting his personality spot on, Icky is a professional showwoman and they both knew him extremely well. Icky worked under him for almost forty years, and Manny for about three times that, plus Manny has mind powers.

And when he did gain control of his body, that control was limited and for a limited amount of time, plus he's probably lost his mind a bit. The accuracy of the performance is not something he'd be terribly concerned with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But… I thought those were just flamboyant, hyperbolic names for some non-anomalous things like a recorder and stuff. I assumed the cabinet was enchanted to never open because a magician never reveals his secrets, and again, the corpse of Fuller is being tugged about by wires. I reiterate that an extremely competent engineer could achieve this in real life.
So, Manny and Icky created these shows, but they display Fuller's personality perfectly, down to the slightest detail. The only thing I could imagine Fuller NOT thinking is "I deserve being in this machine." Corpse Fuller even states that he wants to catch and torture the dynamic duo, which is not something I'd say through the mouth of my archnemesis. I'm surprised they didn't make him say "hurr durr i'm a shoddy pee-tee-barnum nockoff with magic and stuff"- THAT would serve him right.
And during the few minutes when Fuller was able to regain control of his body, he did not TRY to escape, but alluded that he COULD escape if he wanted to. He did not confirm or deny anything said in the script written by OTHER PEOPLE.
EDIT- I read both of those and they were both informative and entertaining. Thank you! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, how much of this machine is magic and how much is non-anomalous technology?

That's kind of weird a question. It wouldn't work without its anomalous components, I can say that at least. It basically records a person's aether waves (magic) with an aetherscope (magic), plays them on a thaumaphone (magic) by sending the waves through thaumically conducting wires (magic) into corpses that still have their souls in them (magic), and the machine itself is protected by an indestructible copper patina (magic) and its internal workings are based on Broken Mechatronics (magic). That's at least seven separate magic components, so it's pretty magical.

And for clarification, the performance was written by Manny, and performed by Icky with some ab libbing and improvisation on her part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. I apologize.
EDIT- Also, how much of this machine is magic and how much is non-anomalous technology? The voices are recorded or something, and the hooked-up Fuller could (probably) be achieved by an extremely competent engineer, which Fuller probably is. And also, was the machine programmed by Veronica and Manny, and thus Herman's performance was orchestrated entirely by them, or something else?
EE- And by the way, regardless of the things I brought up, I upvoted, because it was entertaining and the mental image of P.T. Ballsack's corpse being jerked around by mechanical wires was the most eerie I've had in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Scarlet King, Wondertainment and the Broken God are all SCPs. The fact that they're 001 proposals doesn't make them any less non-canonical because there is no canon. Vincent Anderson is a non-001 SCP, and if someone doesn't like that they're free to ignore it, just as they are this one.

I killed Fuller, yes, but I strongly imply that he could come back. And again, since there is no canon, anyone who wants to write about Herman Fuller as if this doesn't exist is free to do so. I have not ruined the fun for anyone. While it is true that most of the Herman Fuller stuff posted in the past couple of years has at least some input from myself, there are at least a couple I had nothing to do with, and at least one of those (Fish Are Friends Not Clowns) remains firmly unaffiliated with Dread & Circuses. People can still write Herman Fuller stuff that has nothing to do with Dread & Circuses if they want to.

Please read A Real Humdinger and/or There Are No Strings On Me for how Herman managed to survive the Darkness Between Dimensions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You turned Herman Fuller into an SCP.
That's like turning the Scarlet King, or Wondertainment, or the Broken God into an SCP. (You could have at least made it an 001 proposal, if you get what I'm saying.)
And you killed him, too. You ruined all the fun for the Fuller writers. :(
Also, why didn't he disintegrate like Scranton did?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really unsure why this is rated higher, though I will admit that it took me a while to get to it just because its size is daunting. The ideas behind it are great though, the "exposition dump" didn't feel unnatural or bad really, and him being powerful enough to try and break free of the soul trap (and slowly, ever so slowly succeeding) is pretty great. He's a creepy villain. A great creepy villain. I thought that this would go the direction of "Anderson as an SCP" or "Dr. Wondertainment as an SCP", but this really actually works for this character and this description.

The long and short of it is this: +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clever way to avoid having to write out the battle. Writing out a believable battle is difficult at the best of times. A battle between a group of crazy people with a penchant for pulling literal impossible things out of their literal asses and a mysterious evil bugaboo with unknown superpowers and very little characterization beyond being a mysterious evil bugaboo… I just can't see any of that going well.

+1 for a satisfying end to Fuller's story without spoiling his mystery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea of Herman having a theater where people are used as marionettes for being disobedient. It further added to the cruelty of the circus. Though I do have issues with this that lead to my down vote.

It doesn't stand on its own since it requires a bit of context to understand and I believe that SCP articles are to be done first and separate and then be incorporated into canon series instead of being made for a canon so readers can have more foundation for their own head canons. Here it is already established and the narrative is hard to ignore and the only canon that makes sense for one to make is what was provided to them being the tale which sorta leads to my next point.

Herman Fuller is in the article. I feel it's better that Herman is just this mysterious dude we hardly see but just know he is a cruel and cunning guy who feels little to no emotions towards others in the circus. It be better if this article was written if Fuller was still in charge instead of part of the canon it was made for. To have him just suddenly be in an article makes him feel not so special, especially him just defeated. It feels better for such characters to be explored heavily in tales rather than main list SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image Sources:https://static.pexels.com/photos/169972/pexels-photo-169972.jpeg
https://static.pexels.com/photos/7695/night-white-black-contrast.jpg
https://static.pexels.com/photos/15271/pexels-photo-15271.jpg

This SCP is technically a collaboration since the idea for the Matinee was originally that of PeppersGhost, which he shared with me in an email. I fleshed it out and incorporated it into the Dread & Circuses continuity.

Edit: As some of you may be aware, PeppersGhost (creator of Herman Fuller) is not currently an active member of the site. As such my communication with him is very intermittent, and because I really wanted this up for October I posted it without his approval. I should not have done that and I apologize.

Fortunately, Pepper is very reasonable and is willing to let this remain up, but has requested that I acknowledge I acted without his permission. There exists the possibility that he may significantly alter this article or replace it entirely in the future, as is his right.

Thanks to all the beta readers who read this and/or its companion tale:

BananaRepublic
DrMagnus
Flawed
Gold4N (who's either changed their name, deleted their account, or I just wrote their name down wrong)
Ihp
JettG_G
LadyKatie
Modern_Erasmus
MrFrog
Roget
Scorpion451
SheepDude3000
Shio
Sly161
taylor_itkin

And also thanks to Dexanote who pointed out to me that I might have gone a little bit overboard on the beta readers on this one.
